item.title: Chinese embassy clarifies charity event in Dhaka with Jamaat amir
item.description: Chinese embassy in Bangladesh has clarified confusion over its distribution of food packages among Dhaka residents in an event attended by its envoy Yao Wen and Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir Shafiqur Rahman at a school playground in the capital’s Mirpur area on Tuesday morning......

item.title: Local govt elections this year, says Fakhrul
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party secretary general and the local government minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Wednesday said that preparations were under way to hold local government elections, including city corporation polls, within this year......

item.title: Two patients die in SBMCH fire
item.description: Two elderly patients died after a fire broke out at Barishal Sher-e-Bangla Medical College Hospital on Tuesday night......

item.title: Iran hits back as Israel, US continue pounding Iran
item.description: Iran vowed revenge as it prepared the funeral for its security chief Ali Larijani on Wednesday, firing off a wave of missiles against Israel while also reporting fresh Israeli-US strikes across the Islamic Republic......

item.title: 41 defeated candidates challenge February 12 polls results in HC
item.description: Forty-one defeated candidates, mainly from the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party and the main opposition Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami, have challenged the results of the 13th parliamentary election, alleging irregularities and ballot stuffing......Maps

item.title: Another injured Bangladeshi dies in Saudi Arabia
item.description: A Bangladeshi migrant worker injured in an Iranian missile attack in Saudi Arabia died on Tuesday night, raising the death toll of Bangladeshis in the ongoing Middle East conflict to five......

item.title: Short supply keeps many filling stations closed
item.description: The ongoing fuel oil supply shortage in Bangladesh against the backdrop of the growing conflicts in the Gulf region deepened on Wednesday, said consumers......

item.title: Contractual appointments abound in bureaucracy
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government has continued to make contract appointments in key positions of the civil bureaucracy as eight more retired civil servants have already been appointed to the rank of secretary......

item.title: Eid tomorrow if moon sighted today
item.description: Eid-ul-Fitr, one of the biggest religious festivals for the Muslims, will be celebrated in the country tomorrow if the Shawwal crescent moon is sighted today......

item.title: Mad rush for home as Eid exodus peaks
item.description: Thousands of people thronged bus, train and launch terminals across the capital on Wednesday as the Eid-ul-Fitr exodus reached its peak, with holidaymakers heading to their ancestral homes to celebrate the festival with their near and dear ones......Breaking news alerts

item.title: WaterAid hosts workshop on water, sanitation, hygiene
item.description: WaterAid Bangladesh, with the support of the Embassy of Sweden, hosted the national-level workshop for the ‘WASH for UrbanPoor (WASH4UP) phase II’ project on Sunday......

item.title: Confronting most immediate realities
item.description: THE article ‘Likelihood of nuclear strike on Iran’ (New Age, March 2026) by Mohammad Abdur Razzak attempts to present a cautionary scenario. It warns that a conventional war, if it fails to achieve its objectives, could escalate into the unthinkable: the use of nuclear weapons. While framed as a warning, the argument does something more consequential: it elevates a......

item.title: Opportunity or risk for monetary stability?
item.description: BANGLADESH’S central bank is navigating one of the most challenging moments in its recent economic history. Persistent inflation, declining foreign exchange reserves and structural weaknesses in the banking sector have placed monetary policy at the centre of economic stability. At the same time, the global economy is increasingly volatile — shaped by geopolitical......

item.title: Islamophobia and boundaries of democracy
item.description: INTERNATIONAL Day to Combat Islamophobia arrived this year as the United States and Israel deepened military confrontation with Iran — an atmosphere that has pushed anti-Muslim rhetoric from the margins into mainstream western politics. In the United States, several recent statements by elected officials have raised alarms among civil rights advocates......

item.title: Strengthening cooperation
item.description: REGIONAL cooperation has become a strategic necessity in an era marked by economic interdependence, geopolitical rivalry and shared development challenges. For South and Southeast Asia, strengthening collaboration among regional organisations such as the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC), Bay of Bengal Initiative for......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Bhairab reflects river crisis happening all over
item.description: THE River Bhairab that flows through Kachua in Bagerhat faces an existential threat. It has happened over the years because of encroachment, which is widespread, and the discharge of municipal and wet market wastes and untreated sewage, which continues unchecked, directly into the river. The river has become a source of stench for all the time. This has contaminated......

item.title: Multi-pronged approach needed to tackle unemployment
item.description: A WORLD Bank estimate that about half of the youth who entered the labour market in Bangladesh did not find jobs in the past decade comes as a reminder that whatever economic progress the country made did not ensure employment. A World Bank release, issued on March 17 in connection with a three-day visit by the global lender’s vice-president for South Asia......

item.title: Atletico survive Spurs scare to reach Champions League quarters
item.description: Atletico Madrid held off Tottenham's bid for an epic Champions League escape as they reached the quarter-finals despite a 3-2 defeat in the last-16 second leg on Wednesday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Kane and Bayern swat aside Atalanta to set up Real clash
item.description: Harry Kane scored in each half as Bayern Munich thumped Atalanta 4-1 at home in the Champions League last 16 on Wednesday, winning 10-2 on aggregate to book a quarter-final date with Real Madrid....

item.title: Liverpool thrash Galatasaray to reach Champions League quarters
item.description:
Liverpool shrugged off their Premier League malaise to storm into the Champions League quarter-finals by thrashing Galatasaray 4-0 at Anfield on Tuesday.
...

item.title: PSG's Barcola ruled out for several weeks with ankle injury
item.description:
Paris Saint-Germain winger Bradley Barcola has been ruled out for ‘the next few weeks’ with an ankle injury, the Champions League holders said Wednesday.

item.description: Michael Carrick has urged England manager Thomas Tuchel to give Manchester United trio Harry Maguire, Luke Shaw and Kobbie Mainoo a chance to underline their World Cup credentials. ...

item.description:
Enzo Fernandez has suggested he could leave Chelsea at the end of the current season after the London club were knocked out of the Champions League.

item.description: Gianluigi Donnarumma has questioned why Vinicius Junior celebrated his goals at Manchester City on Tuesday in front of the home support, saying a player of his calibre ‘should be loved by everyone’....

item.title: Iran women's football team return home after asylum tussle
item.description: The Iranian women's football team, whose plight has become embroiled in the Middle East war, returned back to Iran on Wednesday where they were promised a welcome ceremony in Tehran....News website membership

item.title: Senegal demands 'corruption' probe over AFCON decision as Morocco defend appeal
item.description: Senegal's government said on Wednesday it wanted an ‘independent international investigation’ into corruption after its national team were sensationally stripped of the Africa Cup of Nations title with the Confederation of African Football (CAF) declaring Morocco champions, two months after their chaotic final....

item.title: 9 killed in road accidents in 6 districts
item.description: At least nine people were killed and several others injured in road accidents in Rajshahi, Bogura, Jhenaidah, Brahmanbaria, Cox’s Bazar and Netrakona districts on Wednesday......

item.description:
Barcelona stormed to a 7-2 victory over Newcastle on Wednesday to reach the Champions League quarter-finals, winning 8-3 on aggregate.

item.description: Professor Mamun Ahmed, pro-vice-chancellor, academic, of Dhaka University, took charge as 15th chairman of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh on Wednesday......Geographic Reference

item.description: A woman was killed and her husband and the driver of a CNG-run auto-rickshaw were seriously injured as a tree fell on the moving vehicle at Baralekha upazila in Moulvibazar on Wednesday morning......

item.description: Japanese ambassador to Bangladesh Saida Shinichi has described the Bangladesh-Japan Economic Partnership Agreement as a significant milestone achieved through sustained dialogue between the two countries......

item.description: The police have recovered the hanging bodies of two friends from their respective houses at Palash upazila in Narsingdi on Tuesday night......

item.description: The dead body of a youth was recovered from a paddy field at Jaintapur upazila in Sylhet on Wednesday noon......Local business directory

item.description: The Rajshahi City Corporation launched a special 15-day crash programme to provide relief to city residents from mosquito infestation on Wednesday......

item.description: Food, fisheries and livestock and agriculture minister Mohammed Amin Ur Rashid at a programme in Cumilla on Wednesday said that initiatives would be taken to introduce crop insurance to protect farmers’ crops from the risk of natural disasters......

item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard rescued 104 people from a launch stranded in the Meghna River in Chandpur on Tuesday......

item.description: Professor Mohammad Al-Forkan of the department of genetic engineering and biotechnology has taken charge as the new vice-chancellor of the University of Chittagong......

item.title: CEGIS, Envolead sign memo to advance climate, sustainability initiatives
item.description: The Envolead Limited and the Centre for Environmental and Geographic Information Services have recently signed a memorandum of understanding at the CEGIS headquarters in Dhaka, strengthening collaboration on climate resilience, environmental sustainability and innovative technology solutions......

item.description: Freedom fighter Shamsuzzohah of Paba Upazila in Rajshahi was laid to eternal rest with state honour on Wednesday......

item.description: Police have arrested a woman for allegedly killing her mother-in-law in Nabiganj upazila of Habiganj on Tuesday......

item.title: Five films set to release for Eid-ul-Fitr
item.description: Five new Bangladeshi films are set to be released in cinemas for Eid-ul-Fitr, one of the largest religious festivals for Muslims, which will be celebrated on either Friday or Saturday depending on the sighting of the moon......

item.description: A group of leaders and activists of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, allegedly harassed two teachers of Jagannath University on the campus Tuesday evening......

item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday placed two accused on a five-day remand each and recorded confessional statements of two others in a case filed over the murder of TikToker Rakibul Islam Rakib in front of the Central Shaheed Minar in the capital......Geographic Reference

item.description: National Citizens Party chief coordinator Nasiruddin Patwary has pledged to avoid personal attacks and unnecessary debates after discussions with senior colleagues,politicalwell-wishers and trusted associates......

item.title: Man, accomplice held over kidnapping son in Chuadanga
item.description: THE police arrested a man and his associate for allegedly kidnapping his own eight-year-old son and demanding ransom at Jibannagar upazila in Chuadanga district on Tuesday night......

item.description: The election commission will send postal ballots to voters residing inside the country by March 25 for the elections to Sherpur-3 and by-polls to Bogura-6 constituencies......

item.description: Professor Mohammad Siddiqur Rahman Khan, former dean of the faculty of arts of Dhaka University, has taken charges as vice-chancellor of the Bangladesh Open University in Gazipur......

item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman will exchange Eid greetings with people from all walks of life, including diplomats and academics, on the occasion of Eid-ul-Fitr, one of the biggest religious festivals of the Muslims......

item.description: The hanging body of a student of Jahangirnagar University was recovered from a residential hall on Wednesday afternoon, university authorities said......

item.title: Ukraine faces missile shortage due to war on Iran: Zelensky
item.description: Volodymyr Zelensky has said Ukraine will face a deficit of missiles it is using to fight Russia due to the war in the Middle East, reports BBConline......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Head of victorious Nepal party hails ‘win for the country’
item.description: The head of the victorious party in Nepal’s election hailed its landslide triumph as a ‘win for the country’ on Wednesday, urging new lawmakers to deliver for people who ‘sacrificed their blood’ in last year’s protests......

item.title: Bus passengers may incur Tk 148cr in excess fares during Eid: JKS
item.description: Whether holidaymakers or homebound, passengers travelling in buses over the Eid-ul-Fitr holidays are slated to shell out an eye-watering Tk 148 crore in excess fares, despite the government’s claims of strict monitoring, according to the Bangladesh Jatri Kalyan Samity, a passengers’ safety advocacy group......

item.description: The government has allocated responsibilities to ministers for conducting parliamentary affairs of various ministries and divisions in the 13th Jatiya Sangsad......

item.title: Prof Mujubar Rahman dies
item.description: Professor Md Mujubar Rahman, former principal of Jashore Michael Madhusudan Dutta College, passed away on Wednesday at a hospital in Khulna. He was 85......

item.title: Expat ministry control room open during Eid amid ME crisis
item.description: In light of the ongoing crisis in the Middle East, the ministry of expatriates’ welfare and overseas employment has decided to keep its control room operational even during the Eid holidays to ensure the safety and overall well-being of Bangladeshi expatriates......

item.title: Trump faces impasse over Iran war
item.description: Having failed to clearly define an objective or exit strategy — and sell the American public on a new war in Iran — president Donald Trump finds himself at an impasse, mere weeks into the conflict......

item.title: Punishment of 30 IU teachers, officials waived
item.description: THE authorities of Islamic University in Kushtia decided to revoke disciplinary actions against 19 teachers and 11 officials who were previously suspended following an investigation into their alleged involvement in activities against the July uprising that ousted the Awami League regime in August 2024......Bangladesh news updates
